root_text.an3.127:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an3.127:1.2: Atha kho hatthako devaputto abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇo kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā:
root_text.an3.127:1.3: “bhagavato purato ṭhassāmī”ti osīdatimeva saṁsīdatimeva, na sakkoti saṇṭhātuṁ.
root_text.an3.127:1.4: Seyyathāpi nāma sappi vā telaṁ vā vālukāya āsittaṁ osīdatimeva saṁsīdatimeva, na saṇṭhāti;
root_text.an3.127:1.6: “bhagavato purato ṭhassāmī”ti osīdatimeva saṁsīdatimeva, na sakkoti saṇṭhātuṁ.
root_text.an3.127:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā hatthakaṁ devaputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.127:2.3: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti, kho hatthako devaputto bhagavato paṭissutvā oḷārikaṁ attabhāvaṁ abhinimminitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.an3.127:2.4: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho hatthakaṁ devaputtaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an3.127:3.1: “Ye te, hatthaka, dhammā pubbe manussabhūtassa pavattino ahesuṁ, api nu te te dhammā etarahi pavattino”ti?
root_text.an3.127:3.2: “Ye ca me, bhante, dhammā pubbe manussabhūtassa pavattino ahesuṁ, te ca me dhammā etarahi pavattino;
root_text.an3.127:3.3: ye ca me, bhante, dhammā pubbe manussabhūtassa nappavattino ahesuṁ, te ca me dhammā etarahi pavattino.
root_text.an3.127:3.4: Seyyathāpi, bhante, bhagavā etarahi ākiṇṇo viharati bhikkhūhi bhikkhunīhi upāsakehi upāsikāhi rājūhi rājamahāmattehi titthiyehi titthiyasāvakehi;
root_text.an3.127:3.5: evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, ākiṇṇo viharāmi devaputtehi.
root_text.an3.127:3.6: Dūratopi, bhante, devaputtā āgacchanti hatthakassa devaputtassa santike ‘dhammaṁ sossāmā’ti.
root_text.an3.127:3.7: Tiṇṇāhaṁ, bhante, dhammānaṁ atitto appaṭivāno kālaṅkato.
root_text.an3.127:3.9: Bhagavato ahaṁ, bhante, dassanassa atitto appaṭivāno kālaṅkato;
root_text.an3.127:3.11: saṅghassāhaṁ, bhante, upaṭṭhānassa atitto appaṭivāno kālaṅkato.
root_text.an3.127:3.12: Imesaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ atitto appaṭivāno kālaṅkatoti.

translation_text.an3.127:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an3.127:1.2: Then, late at night, the glorious godling Hatthaka, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha. Thinking,
translation_text.an3.127:1.3: “I will stand before the Buddha,” he sank and melted down, and wasn’t able to stay still.
translation_text.an3.127:1.4: It’s like when ghee or oil is poured on sand, it sinks and melts down, and can’t remain stable.
translation_text.an3.127:2.1: Then the Buddha said to Hatthaka,
translation_text.an3.127:2.3: “Yes, sir,” replied Hatthaka. He manifested a solid incarnation, bowed to the Buddha, and stood to one side.
translation_text.an3.127:3.1: “Hatthaka, I wonder whether you still promulgate now the teachings that you promulgated when you were a human being?”
translation_text.an3.127:3.2: “I still promulgate the teachings that I promulgated as a human being.
translation_text.an3.127:3.3: And I also promulgate teachings that I didn’t promulgate as a human being.
translation_text.an3.127:3.4: Just as the Buddha these days lives crowded by monks, nuns, laymen, and laywomen; by rulers and their chief ministers, and monastics of other religions and their disciples,
translation_text.an3.127:3.5: so I live crowded by the godlings.
translation_text.an3.127:3.6: The godlings come from far away, thinking, ‘We’ll hear the teaching in the presence of Hatthaka.’
translation_text.an3.127:3.7: Sir, I passed away without getting enough of three things.
translation_text.an3.127:3.12: I passed away without getting enough of these three things.
translation_text.an3.127:5.2: loving to hear the true teaching,
translation_text.an3.127:5.3: Hatthaka has gone to the Aviha realm
translation_text.an3.127:5.4: without getting enough of these three things.”

variant_text.an3.127:1.3: osīdatimeva saṁsīdatimeva → osīdati ceva saṁsīdati ca (bj); osīdati saṁsīdati (sya-all, km); osīdati ceva saṁsīdati ceva (pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.127:4.2: Tittimajjhagā → titti addhā (sya-all); tittiṁ ajjha (pts1ed); titti tittisambhavaṁ (mr)
